name: Install-Package

options.name: -AcceptLicense
options.description: AcceptLicense automatically accepts the license agreement during installation.

options.name: -AllowClobber
options.description: Overrides warning messages about conflicts with existing commands. Overwrites existing commands that have the same name as commands being installed.

options.name: -AllowPrereleaseVersions
options.description: Allows the installation of packages marked as prerelease.

options.name: -AllVersions
options.description: `Install-Package` installs all available versions of the package. By default, only the newest version is installed.

options.name: -Command
options.description: Specifies one or more commands that `Install-Package` searches.

options.name: -ConfigFile
options.description: Specifies a path that contains a configuration file.

options.name: -Contains
options.description: `Install-Package` gets objects if the Contains parameter specifies a value that matches any of the object's property values.

options.name: -Credential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to access the computer and run commands. Type a user name, such as User01 , Domain01\User01 , or enter a PSCredential object, generated by the `Get-Credential` cmdlet. If you type a user name, you're prompted for a password.

options.name: -Destination
options.description: Specifies a path to an input object.

options.name: -DscResource
options.description: Specifies one or more Desired State Configuration (DSC) resources that are searched by `Install-Package`. Use the `Find-DscResource` cmdlet to find DSC resources.

options.name: -ExcludeVersion
options.description: Switch to exclude the version number in the folder path.

options.name: -Filter
options.description: Specifies terms to search for within the Name and Description properties.

options.name: -FilterOnTag
options.description: Specifies a tag that filters results and excludes results that don't contain the specified tag.

options.name: -Force
options.description: Forces the command to run without asking for user confirmation. Overrides restrictions that prevent `Install-Package` from succeeding, with the exception of security.

options.name: -ForceBootstrap
options.description: Forces PackageManagement to automatically install the package provider for the specified package.

options.name: -Headers
options.description: Specifies the package headers.

options.name: -Includes
options.description: Specifies whether `Install-Package` should find all package types. The acceptable values for this parameter are as follows:

options.name: -InputObject
options.description: Accepts pipeline input. Specifies a package by using the package's SoftwareIdentity type. `Find-Package` outputs a SoftwareIdentity object.

options.name: -InstallUpdate
options.description: Indicates that `Install-Package` installs updates.

options.name: -MaximumVersion
options.description: Specifies the maximum allowed package version that you want to install. If you don't specify this parameter, `Install-Package` installs the package's newest version.

options.name: -MinimumVersion
options.description: Specifies the minimum allowed package version that you want to install. If you don't add this parameter, `Install-Package` installs the package's newest version that satisfies any version specified by the MaximumVersion parameter.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies one or more package names. Multiple package names must be separated by commas.

options.name: -NoPathUpdate
options.description: NoPathUpdate only applies to the `Install-Script` cmdlet. NoPathUpdate is a dynamic parameter added by the provider and isn't supported by `Install-Package`.

options.name: -PackageManagementProvider
options.description: Specifies the name of the PackageManagement provider.

name: -ProviderName
-Provider
options.description: Specifies one or more package provider names to which to scope your package search. You can get package provider names by running the `Get-PackageProvider` cmdlet.

options.name: -Proxy
options.description: Specifies a proxy server for the request, rather than connecting directly to an internet resource.

options.name: -ProxyCredential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to use the proxy server specified by the Proxy parameter.

options.name: -PublishLocation
options.description: Specifies the path to a package's published location.

options.name: -RequiredVersion
options.description: Specifies the exact allowed version of the package that you want to install. If you don't add this parameter, `Install-Package` installs the package's newest version that satisfies any version specified by the MaximumVersion parameter.

options.name: -RoleCapability
options.description: Specifies an array of role capabilities.

options.name: -Scope
options.description: Specifies the scope for which to install the package. The acceptable values for this parameter are as follows:

options.name: -ScriptPublishLocation
options.description: Specifies the path to a script's published location.

options.name: -ScriptSourceLocation
options.description: Specifies the script source location.

options.name: -SkipDependencies
options.description: Skips the installation of software dependencies.

options.name: -SkipPublisherCheck
options.description: Allows you to get a package version that is newer than your installed version. For example, an installed package that is digitally signed by a trusted publisher but a new version isn't digitally signed.

options.name: -SkipValidate
options.description: Switch that skips validating the credentials of a package.

options.name: -Source
options.description: Specifies one or more package sources. Multiple package source names must be separated by commas. You can get package source names by running the `Get-PackageSource` cmdlet.

options.name: -Tag
options.description: Specifies one or more strings to search for in the package metadata.

options.name: -Type
options.description: Specifies whether to search for packages with a module, a script, or both. The acceptable values for this parameter are as follows:

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if `Install-Package` cmdlet is run. The cmdlet is not run.

name: -Debug
-db
options.description: Displays programmer-level detail about the operation done by the command. This parameter works only when the command generates a debugging message.

name: -ErrorAction
-ea
options.description: Determines how the cmdlet responds to a non-terminating error from the command. This parameter works only when the command generates a non-terminating error.

name: -ErrorVariable
-ev
options.description: Error records are automatically stored in the `$Error` automatic variable. When you use the ErrorVariable parameter on a command, PowerShell also stores the error records emitted by the command in the variable specified by the parameter.

name: -InformationAction
-infa
options.description: Within the command or script in which it's used, the InformationAction common parameter overrides the value of the `$InformationPreference` preference variable, which by default is set to SilentlyContinue.

name: -InformationVariable
-iv
options.description: When you use the InformationVariable common parameter, information records are stored in the variable specified by the parameter.

name: -OutBuffer
-ob
options.description: Determines the number of objects to accumulate in a buffer before any objects are sent through the pipeline. If you omit this parameter, objects are sent as they're generated.

name: -OutVariable
-ov
options.description: Stores output objects from the command in the specified variable in addition to sending the output along the pipeline.

name: -PipelineVariable
-pv
options.description: PipelineVariable allows access to the most recent value passed into the next pipeline segment by the command that uses this parameter. Any command in the pipeline can access the value using the named PipelineVariable. The value is assigned to the variable when it's passed into the next pipeline segment.

name: -ProgressAction
-proga
options.description: Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. The Write-Progress cmdlet creates progress bars that show a command's status.

name: -Verbose
-vb
options.description: Displays detailed information about the operation done by the command. This information resembles the information in a trace or in a transaction log. This parameter works only when the command generates a verbose message.

name: -WarningAction
-wa
options.description: Determines how the cmdlet responds to a warning from the command. Continue is the default value. This parameter works only when the command generates a warning message.

name: -WarningVariable
-wv
options.description: Stores warning records about the command in the specified variable.

description: Installs one or more software packages.
